subject: [PATCH] mm: thp: unlock i_mmap before releasing split folios

reviews.author: Hao Zhang
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Hao Zhang pointed out a potential issue where the lock_at page is still locked and in the page cache, causing inode eviction to fail after truncate_inode_pages_final() until __folio_split() has released i_mmap_rwsem. He suggested that this can be hit if lock_at ends up in an after-split folio beyond EOF and __folio_freeze_and_split_unmapped() removes it from the page cache.

Reviewer Hao Zhang pointed out that the patch does not address a potential issue where lock_at pages are still locked and in the page cache after folio splitting, which can lead to inode eviction failure due to the i_mmap_rwsem lock. He provided a crash report as evidence and requested that the actual crash reports be shared before making any code changes.

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes pointed out that the patch description analysis is incorrect and requested crash reports to investigate the situation further.

Reviewer requested additional evidence and context to support the patch, specifically asking for reproducer, .config file, architecture details, and crash reports to verify the issue.
reviews.raw_body:
I hate to have to keep pointing this out to people and maybe you were trying to
limit the mailing list rather to maintainers only buuuut :)

It definitely helps to cc everybody relevant when it comes to getting review.

On Fri, Jul 10, 2026 at 11:56:00PM +0800, Hao Zhang wrote:

---

Thanks but do you have any of the other pieces around this to help? Like a
reproducer, .config, the arch you ran it on, etc. etc.?

In general please do ensure you evidence things like this without needing to be
asked for it, we can't just take your word for it and the more you do to make
our lives easier the quicker we can get the review done.

We've had real issues in the past with private syzbot reports where it's turned
out on more than one occasion to not actually be a genuine issue.

So your help at a time where mm review is _totally_ overwhelmed would be much
appreciated :)

Thanks, Lorenzo

reviews.author: Kiryl Shutsemau (author)
reviews.summary: The author acknowledges that the patch does not fully address the issue, as shmem_uncharge() also touches the inode in a freed window. They propose an alternative fix by pinning the inode explicitly using igrab(), and provide a patch to test.

2026-07-10.patch_summary: This patch addresses a potential issue where inode eviction is blocked due to an unlocked i_mmap lock, which prevents the page cache from being freed. The problem arises when a split folio is released and its i_mmap lock is not properly dropped before the inode eviction process completes. To fix this, the patch ensures that the i_mmap lock is unlocked before releasing the split folios, allowing the inode eviction to proceed as expected.
